sanskrit:
न हि कश्चित् क्षणमपि जातु तिष्ठत्यकर्मकृत्।
कार्यते ह्यवशः कर्म सर्वः प्रकृतिजैर्गुणैः॥
transliteration:
na hi kaścit kṣaṇam api jātu tiṣṭhaty akarma-kṛt
kāryate hy avaśaḥ karma sarvaḥ prakṛti-jair guṇaiḥ
literal_meaning: No one ever remains inactive even for a moment; all are compelled to act helplessly by the qualities born of material nature.
contextual_meaning: The mind is never truly still; even 'doing nothing' is a form of action shaped by unconscious drives. True choice lies not in stopping action but in aligning it with higher purpose.
psychological_state: Hyper-arousal masked as passivity; the anxious belief that constant motion equals safety.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling social media for hours while telling yourself you're 'taking a break,' then feeling drained yet unable to stop.
anchor_text: You are always acting—choose actions that serve your highest self.
